audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:time + verb + subject + ...]]. When a time phrase comes first, keep the action word second and put the subject after it.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces Dutch main-clause V2 after a clause-initial time phrase: [[grammar:time + finite verb + subject + ...]].

audienceSections.normal:
#### time + action + person

Say the time first in [[grammar:Om negen uur vertrekt Mark ...]]. The action [[grammar:vertrekt]] comes before [[grammar:Mark]].
audienceSections.expert:
#### time + verb + subject + ...

When [[grammar:Om negen uur]] fills first position, finite [[grammar:vertrekt]] occupies second position and subject [[grammar:Mark]] follows. The same order appears after [[grammar:Daarna]] and [[grammar:In de middag]].

audienceSections.normal: Compare [[grammar:Mark vertrekt om negen uur.]] with [[grammar:Om negen uur vertrekt Mark naar het station.]]
audienceSections.expert: The contrast shows subject-first order when the subject is initial and inversion after a non-subject initial constituent. Separable [[grammar:komen ... terug]] remains supporting vocabulary.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Het is ochtend.]] sets the time.
- [[grammar:Mark heeft een afspraak in de stad.]] says why Mark is going into town.
- [[grammar:Het adres is in de agenda.]] says where the address is.
- [[grammar:Om negen uur vertrekt Mark naar het station.]] puts the time first.
- [[grammar:Daan gaat met Mark.]] says who goes with him.
- [[grammar:Om tien uur begint de afspraak.]] gives the appointment time.
- [[grammar:Daarna lopen Mark en Daan naar de markt.]] says what happens next.
- [[grammar:In de middag komen zij terug.]] says when they return.
- [[grammar:Thuis drinkt Mark koffie en Daan leest een boek.]] describes the two actions at home.
- [[grammar:Het plan klopt.]] confirms that the plan is correct.
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Het is ochtend.]] is a temporal setting frame.
- [[grammar:Mark heeft een afspraak in de stad.]] uses inherited [[grammar:hebben]] with a locative complement.
- [[grammar:Het adres is in de agenda.]] is a copular locative clause.
- [[grammar:Om negen uur vertrekt Mark naar het station.]] and [[grammar:Om tien uur begint de afspraak.]] place a time phrase first, finite verb second, and subject after the verb.
- [[grammar:Daan gaat met Mark.]] retains ordinary subject-first order.
- [[grammar:Daarna lopen Mark en Daan naar de markt.]] and [[grammar:In de middag komen zij terug.]] provide additional non-subject-first V2 examples.
- [[grammar:Thuis drinkt Mark koffie en Daan leest een boek.]] combines clause-initial locative V2 with coordination.
- [[grammar:Het plan klopt.]] uses the restricted “be correct” sense of [[grammar:kloppen]].
